explanations.2: This class implements the Iterator interface, which means it promises to provide methods like current(), next(), and rewind() so you can loop through its items.
explanations.6: The variable $dataobject contains a class name as a string, and new $dataobject() creates a new instance of that class dynamically.
explanations.7: This loop takes each key-value pair from $item and assigns them directly as properties on the object, like $object->name = 'John'.
